title: Make native GUI cargo build launch deterministic without test-feature unification
operator_request: Follow up the launch correction: the native GUI should launch from a normal current-source cargo build, not from stale artifacts or accidental test feature unification.

state_recovery.recovery_rule: If a no-context model resumes this MT, reproduce the bug with a fresh cargo build target outside the repo. Do not rely on ../Handshake_Artifacts/handshake-native-target or any target previously built by tests. Do not edit unrelated inherited diagnostics or Palmistry files.

scope.summary: Fix the native GUI crate's runtime feature declaration so `cargo build --manifest-path src/frontend/handshake_native/Cargo.toml --bin handshake-native` produces an executable that launches on Windows without first running a test build.
base_scope: Inspect the native GUI cargo feature graph for the missing wgpu platform backend feature.
Add the smallest explicit runtime dependency feature needed for Windows launch.
Keep build/test artifacts outside the repo under ../Handshake_Artifacts/.
Verify a fresh non-test cargo build launches the GUI from the current-source target.
acceptance_criteria: Fresh cargo build of the native GUI bin succeeds into an MT-owned target directory under ../Handshake_Artifacts/.
Launching the resulting handshake-native.exe no longer fails with 'No wgpu backend feature that is implemented for the target platform was enabled'.
The launched GUI process path points at the MT-owned current-source target, not the stale shared target.
The Handshake backend remains healthy and the GUI connects to it.
The fix is explicit in product build configuration and does not require running `cargo test --no-run` first.
proof_targets: Get-Process proves the GUI path is the MT-005 target and the main window is present.
stderr log is empty or at least does not contain the wgpu backend feature panic.

implementation_plan: 1. Use cargo tree/metadata or the lockfile to identify which wgpu platform backend feature was only present under dev/test feature unification.
2. Patch the native GUI Cargo.toml with the smallest explicit runtime feature declaration.
3. Build the native GUI bin into a fresh MT-owned target directory under ../Handshake_Artifacts/.
4. Relaunch the GUI from that target and verify process path, window presence, backend health, and no wgpu backend panic.
5. Update MT-005 closeout and mt_index before moving to MT-006.

commands_planned: Patch native GUI runtime dependency features.
Build into a fresh MT-owned target directory.
Launch the fresh executable and verify process path, stderr, window, and backend health.

results: Feature proof before/after patch: `wgpu/dx12` is now pulled by the runtime `handshake-native` package, not only by dev dependency `egui_kittest`.
Fresh GUI launch passed: running process PID 26328 path is `../Handshake_Artifacts/ckc-overhaul-launch/native-target-mt005/debug/handshake-native.exe`.
Window proof passed: main window title `Handshake`, nonzero main window handle.
stderr proof passed: `native-run-mt005-20260628-185606.err.log` length is 0; no wgpu backend feature panic.
Backend health proof passed: `/health` returned status ok, component handshake_core, db_status ok, migration_version 2026.

risk_notes.risk: The fix hides the problem by reusing a target already warmed by test builds.
risk_notes.failure_scenario: A later clean machine still crashes on launch because the runtime dependency features are incomplete.
risk_notes.mitigation: Use a fresh MT-owned target directory and do not run `cargo test --no-run` before the proof build.

risk_notes.risk: Enabling too many graphics backends increases build size or platform behavior.
risk_notes.failure_scenario: A broad default-feature restore pulls unrelated renderer or platform features.
risk_notes.mitigation: Prefer the minimal Windows wgpu backend feature required by the locked wgpu/eframe graph.

handoff.summary: Native GUI build hotfix completed. The Windows wgpu backend is explicit in runtime Cargo features, and a fresh non-test build launches from the MT-owned target without the previous wgpu backend panic.

risks: A GUI launch can appear successful from a stale executable while the current-source build remains broken.
Target reuse can mask feature graph bugs.
Build config changes can accidentally pull dev-only dependencies into runtime.
minimum_controls: Check stderr for the exact wgpu backend panic.
